text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 18 April 2021 

Dr Anita Kapoor  
Riverside Medical Practice  
14 New Cavendish Street, London W1G 8TU  

Dear Dr Anita Kapoor,  

Daniel Morton, DOB: 22-09-1983, Hospital No: U921774 NHS No. 6384729105  
Flat 3, 27 Brookfield Road, London, N5 1ES 

Summary/ Diagnoses: Generalised epilepsy following prior stress-related exacerbations/improved wellbeing with recent travel and relaxation/previous mild anxiety symptoms now settling/osteopenia (stable)/non-smoker/abstains from alcohol. 

Medication: 
- Sodium Valproate 500 mg twice daily. 
- Lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily. 
- Cholecalciferol 1000 IU once daily. 
- Omeprazole 20 mg once daily.  

I reviewed Daniel Morton in clinic today, attending alone and reporting continued improvement in overall health. He describes that a period of structured relaxation and short local travel breaks since last summer has supported better sleep, lower stress levels, and a more regular routine. He has resumed light exercise (brisk walking 20–30 minutes most days) without adverse effects. Appetite is good and weight stable. He denies mood disturbance and scores within normal range on brief screening. 

Seizures: The patient has generalised epilepsy. He has been using a smartwatch and home seizure-detection application linked to his diary. Wearable/device logs show no seizures since 12 June 2020. He also reports no witnessed events, no morning myalgias suggestive of nocturnal convulsions, and no tongue biting or post-ictal confusion. No missed doses reported in the last three months. 

Examination: Alert and oriented; speech fluent; cranial nerves intact; tone, power and coordination normal; gait steady. Cardiovascular and respiratory examinations unremarkable. No tremor or ataxia. 

Investigations: Most recent liver function tests and full blood count from February 2021 were within reference ranges. Vitamin D sufficient on supplementation. 

Plan: 
- Continue current antiseizure medication regimen unchanged given sustained seizure freedom and clinical improvement. 
- Reinforce adherence and ongoing use of wearable/device logging. 
- Routine bloods (LFTs, FBC) to be repeated at 6–12 months as per valproate monitoring schedule. 
- Safety advice revisited; no driving until eligible under DVLA guidance, but given ongoing seizure freedom, we will reassess timelines at next review. 
- Follow-up in neurology clinic in 6 months or earlier if concerns arise. 

Overall, Daniel Morton is clinically well, with improving wellbeing and stability. The history, examination and objective logs all support ongoing seizure freedom and recovery. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: Wearable/device logs show no seizures since 12 June 2020.
Wearable/device logs show no seizures since 12 June 2020

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states the patient has had no seizures since 12 June 2020, with corroboration from wearable/device logs and history. Clinic date is 18 April 2021. Calculate duration of seizure freedom: from 12 June 2020 to 18 April 2021 is 10 months and 6 days (June 12 to July 12 = 1 month; then to April 12 = 10 months total; plus 6 days). This exceeds 6 months, so classify as seizure free for 10 months (rounding to months as per allowed formats).
